_README: RECONCILED facts.json for thailandforamericans — the ONLY facts file content stages read. Built from 3 AI sources (claude+gemini+deepseek) reconciled semantically per hard rule 2. serve_live fields never frozen. verify_recommended = YMYL/change-prone, confirm against official source before publishing prose. Recommendation pools: 'core' = named by >=2 sources.
_fact_field_shape: { value, source_url, status, notes }. serve_live values are null by design.

name_local.value: Prathet Thai (ประเทศไทย); official: Kingdom of Thailand (Ratcha-anachak Thai)

value: Thai
ICT (UTC+7)
timezones.notes: No daylight saving; ~11–12h ahead of US Eastern depending on US DST.

power_plug_voltage.value: 220V, 50Hz; plug types A/B/C (US flat 2-prong Type A/B fits common sockets; bring an adapter for round-pin/grounded plugs and a converter for non-dual-voltage devices)
power_plug_voltage.notes: All 3 sources agree 220–230V/50Hz; 220V is the nominal Thai figure.

visa._note: YMYL + actively changing in 2026. NEVER freeze the day-count in prose.

type.notes: Americans are on the visa-exemption list — no visa for short tourism.

max_stay_days.notes: IN FLUX: 60 days at the border currently; Cabinet approved (19 May 2026) reverting most nationalities incl. US to 30 days, NOT yet in force pending Royal Gazette publication. Officers apply the rule in force on the entry date. Serve live / tell readers to verify on their travel date — do NOT freeze a number.

online_authorization_required.value: Yes — Thailand Digital Arrival Card (TDAC), mandatory for all foreign arrivals (air/land/sea), filed online within 72h before arrival. FREE on the official portal. Separate from a visa.
online_authorization_required.notes: Replaced the paper TM6. Beware third-party sites charging a fee.

extension.value: Visa-exempt entry can usually be extended once by 30 days at a Thai immigration office; fee commonly cited ~1,900 THB. Apply before current permission expires.

value: Any 'TDAC' site that charges a fee (the official TDAC is free)
Unofficial 'visa-run' brokers who take your passport
Fake Thailand e-Visa / arrival-card lookalike domains

passport_validity_required.value: At least 6 months beyond your arrival date (recommended and can be enforced).

onward_ticket_required.value: Proof of onward/return travel within the permitted stay may be requested by airlines or immigration.

proof_of_funds.value: For visa exemption / tourist visa: 20,000 THB per person or 40,000 THB per family (reinstated May 2025, applies 2026). Random checks; cash or recent bank statement. (The 10,000/20,000 figure is the separate Visa-on-Arrival tier, which does NOT apply to US passports.)

notes.value: Requirements can change with little notice — verify with the Thai MFA / nearest Thai embassy on your actual travel date.

approx_rate_note.notes: VOLATILE — USD/THB moves daily. Never freeze; serve a live rate or omit a number.

cash_vs_card.value: Cash-dominated, especially outside Bangkok and for street food, markets, tuk-tuks, small/rural vendors. Cards accepted at hotels, malls, chain/upscale restaurants and many city venues. PromptPay QR is mainly for Thai bank accounts.

atm_availability.value: ATMs ubiquitous in cities and tourist areas and accept major US networks (Visa/Mastercard). Thai ATMs add a per-withdrawal foreign-card fee (commonly ~220 THB) on top of your home bank's fee — withdraw larger amounts less often.

currency_import_export_limit.notes: DISPUTED across sources (USD 15k vs USD 20k vs 450k THB). Reality roughly: no limit on incoming foreign currency but large sums (~USD 15–20k equiv) should be declared, and there are limits on exporting THB. Verify current official thresholds before writing a number — do not freeze.

tipping_norm.value: Not obligatory and not a strong traditional custom, but appreciated and increasingly expected in tourist areas: round up taxis, ~20–50 THB for porters, leave small change at restaurants. Many upscale venues add a 10% service charge.

value: Suvarnabhumi (BKK) — Bangkok main hub
Don Mueang (DMK) — Bangkok low-cost
Phuket (HKT)
Chiang Mai (CNX)
Koh Samui (USM)
Krabi (KBV)

flight_from_audience_origins.notes: Typically 1 stop from the US via East Asia (Tokyo/Seoul/Hong Kong/Taipei) or the Middle East (Doha/Dubai); ~20–24h total from US hubs. PRICES serve_live — never freeze. Verify current nonstop/route options.

intercity_rail.value: State Railway of Thailand (SRT). Atmospheric, slower than flights/buses. Highlight: the Bangkok–Chiang Mai overnight sleeper; southern line to Surat Thani for the Gulf islands.

buses.value: Extensive intercity network from basic local buses to VIP/first-class and overnight coaches; minivans connect smaller towns. Use government BKS or reputable operators.

domestic_flights.value: Cheap, fast, frequent. Carriers: Thai AirAsia, Thai Lion Air, Nok Air, Bangkok Airways (owns Samui), Thai Vietjet, Thai Airways. Most north/south hops are 1–1.5h vs 10+h overland.

ride_sharing.value: Grab is the dominant ride-hail app (cars, taxis, motorbike taxis, food); Bolt and inDrive also operate. Cheaper and clearer than negotiating tuk-tuks. Bangkok also has BTS Skytrain + MRT metro.

ferries_islands.value: Islands reached by ferry/speedboat from mainland piers (Krabi/Phuket piers for the Andaman islands; Surat Thani/Donsak for Koh Samui/Phangan/Tao). Combined bus+boat and flight+boat tickets common. Andaman (west) and Gulf (east) coasts have OPPOSITE peak seasons.

car_rental_and_driving.value: Thais drive on the LEFT. An International Driving Permit + home license is required. City traffic is heavy; many tourists use Grab/drivers instead. Island scooter rental is common but a major injury/scam risk and often not covered by insurance without a proper motorcycle license.

climate_by_season.value: Tropical with three broad seasons for most of the country: cool & dry (~Nov–Feb, peak/best), hot (~Mar–May), rainy/monsoon (~Jun–Oct). The GULF islands (Samui/Phangan/Tao) run on a different cycle — wettest ~Oct–Dec — so they can be good when the rest is rainy. The North is cooler in the cool season.

value: November
December
January
February
best_months.notes: Cool, dry, peak season nationally (also peak prices/crowds).

value: Songkran (~April 13–15) if you dislike crowds/water fights — though it's also a highlight
Peak Andaman monsoon (~Sep–Oct)
Northern burning season (~Feb–Apr) when Chiang Mai air quality drops sharply

safety_health._note: YMYL. Advisory level change-prone; emergency numbers stable.

advisory_level.notes: Sources agree: US State Dept Level 1 (Exercise Normal Precautions) across most of Thailand, with HIGHER warnings for the deep-south provinces (Yala, Pattani, Narathiwat, Songkhla). Verify current level + date before publishing — do not freeze.

main_risk.value: Road-traffic accidents — especially motorbikes — are the leading cause of tourist injury/death. Also: bar-tab/gem/tour scams, petty theft/pickpocketing, methanol-tainted bootleg alcohol, riptides on some beaches, dengue (mosquito-borne).

vaccines.value: No vaccines legally required for entry from the US (unless arriving from a yellow-fever country). CDC commonly recommends being up to date on routine vaccines plus Hepatitis A and Typhoid for most travelers; Hep B, Japanese encephalitis, and rabies for some. See a travel clinic 4–6 weeks ahead.

tap_water_safe.value: No — don't drink the tap water. Bottled water is cheap and universal; ice in established venues is generally factory-made and fine.

emergency_numbers.notes: Tourist Police (1155) are English-speaking. All sources agree ambulance 1669, fire 199.

value: 'The Grand Palace/temple is closed today' → tuk-tuk gem/tailor detour
Tuk-tuk/taxi refusing the meter or quoting inflated flat fares
Jet-ski / motorbike rental damage claims
Exorbitant bar tabs + surprise cover charges with intimidation
Overpriced 'gem' shops
Fake TDAC/visa fee sites

sim_esim.value: Cheap, easy local data. Networks: AIS, TrueMove H, dtac. Buy a tourist SIM at the airport or set up an eSIM (Airalo, Holafly, etc.) before you land. Tourist data packages (8–30 days) are inexpensive.

coverage.value: Excellent 4G/5G in cities and most tourist areas; weaker on remote islands and in the far-north mountains.

costs._note: RECOMMENDATION-ish, approximate, dated; serve_live so never frozen as fact. Thailand is inexpensive by US standards.

daily_budget_low.notes: Backpacker/budget: very roughly USD 30–50/day. Indicative only — verify live.

daily_budget_mid.notes: Mid-range: very roughly USD 75–150/day. Indicative only.

dress_norms.value: Cover shoulders and knees to enter temples (wats); remove shoes before entering temple buildings and homes. Keep beachwear at the beach. Generally relaxed, but modest dress is respected.

value: NEVER disrespect the King/monarchy — lèse-majesté is a serious crime
Don't touch anyone's head; don't point your feet at people or Buddha images
Return a 'wai' (palms-together greeting) respectfully
Remove shoes where indicated
Stand for the royal anthem where it's played
Keep your cool — public anger / causing someone to 'lose face' is deeply frowned upon
Don't buy Buddha images as décor (legally/culturally sensitive)

how_to_get_there.value: Main international gateway. Suvarnabhumi (BKK) for full-service carriers; Don Mueang (DMK) for low-cost. Airport Rail Link + taxi/Grab into the city.

local_transport.value: BTS Skytrain + MRT metro (clean, cheap, beat traffic), Chao Phraya river boats, Grab, metered taxis, tuk-tuks (negotiate/avoid for value), motorbike taxis.

value.name: Grand Palace & Wat Phra Kaew (Emerald Buddha)
value.why: The most iconic temple complex in Thailand; stunning architecture and national treasure.

value.name: Wat Pho (Reclining Buddha)
value.why: Huge reclining Buddha and traditional Thai massage school; just south of Grand Palace.

value.name: Wat Arun (Temple of Dawn)
value.why: Famous riverside temple featuring towers decorated with intricately patterned porcelain shards.

value.name: Chatuchak Weekend Market
value.why: One of the world's largest open-air weekend markets, hosting over 15,000 diverse shopping stalls.

value.name: Chinatown (Yaowarat)
value.why: Legendary evening street-food crawl.

value.name: Jim Thompson House
value.why: Traditional teak house + silk story.

value.who_for: First-timers & nightlife; on the BTS, endless dining.

value.why: Famous for crab omelet and drunken noodles; long waits but worth it.
The classic evening food crawl.
Cheap, iconic.
Skyline dining splurge.
Must-try Thai dessert.
Ancient capital with impressive temple ruins; 1–1.5 hrs by train or minivan.
Classic floating-market experience.
WWII history + nature.
UNESCO site with waterfalls and wildlife; 2.5 hrs drive.

how_to_get_there.value: Fly CNX from Bangkok (~1h15) or overnight sleeper train / bus (~10–12h).

local_transport.value: Songthaews (red shared trucks), Grab, tuk-tuks, rented bicycles/scooters; the old city is walkable.

value.name: Wat Phra That Doi Suthep
value.why: Mountain-top temple with panoramic views; iconic northern Thai architecture.

value.name: Old City Temples (Wat Chedi Luang, Wat Phra Singh)
value.why: Stunning Lanna-style temples in the historic moated center.

value.name: Sunday Walking Street
value.why: Massive night market closing down the center of Old Town for local handicrafts, art, and incredible northern street food.

value.name: Elephant Nature Park
value.why: A globally recognized, pioneering ethical sanctuary dedicated to rescuing abused logging and tourism elephants.

value.name: Doi Inthanon National Park
value.why: Thailand's highest peak, waterfalls, twin pagodas.

value.who_for: Perfect for first-timers wanting walkability to historic temples, dense markets, and cute cafes inside the ancient square moat.
Trendy cafes, shopping, nightlife; modern hotels and condos.
Shopping and nightlife; convenient but busy.

value.name_or_type: Khao Soi (local specialty) — try Khao Soi Khun Yai
value.why: Creamy coconut curry noodle soup; a northern Thai icon.
Lanna specialties.
Chiang Mai is a top place to take one.
Great street food with grilled meats, sai ua (sausage), and sticky rice.

value.place: Chiang Rai (White Temple, Blue Temple, Black House)
value.why: Unique contemporary temples; 3–4 hrs drive or tour.
Bohemian mountain town with hot springs and waterfalls; 3–4 hrs winding road.
Stand at the highest peak in Thailand, experience mist-clad cloud forests, hike scenic nature trails, and view roaring waterfalls.

how_to_get_there.value: Fly HKT (direct from Bangkok ~1h25 and some intl routes) or bus+ferry. Largest Andaman-coast island, road-connected to the mainland.

local_transport.value: Grab (limited in places due to local taxi politics), metered/negotiated taxis, songthaews, rented scooters; the island is large — distances are real.

value.name: Old Phuket Town
value.why: Sino-Portuguese streets, cafés, murals — more character than the beach strips.

value.name: Phang Nga Bay (James Bond Island)
value.why: Spectacular boat excursions navigating dramatic vertical limestone karsts rising straight out of emerald-green water.

value.name: Big Buddha (Wat Chalong)
value.why: Massive seated Buddha with panoramic views.

value.name: Beaches (Kata, Karon, Freedom, Nai Harn)
value.why: Range from lively to quiet.

value.name: Patong Beach
value.why: Most famous beach with nightlife, water sports, and dining.

value.name: Phi Phi Islands (day trip)
value.why: Stunning limestone cliffs, Maya Bay, snorkeling; crowded but iconic.

value.who_for: Exclusively for those prioritizing intense nightlife, clubs, neon lights, and lively crowds along Bangla Road.
Culture, food, boutique hotels; no beach.

value.why: Best food on the island.
Pick-and-grill.
Fresh catch grilled on the beach; pick your fish.
Trendy night market with food trucks and local dishes.

value.name_or_type: Fine dining at Kata Rocks or Sri Panwa
value.why: High-end views and cuisine; special occasions.
Board a fast speedboat or catamaran to snorkel pristine turquoise waters and view Maya Bay, made famous by the movies.
Limestone karsts, sea canoeing; 1.5 hrs drive+boat.
Top diving/snorkeling (seasonal, closed in monsoon).
Quieter islands for snorkeling and relaxation; 30–60 min boat.

how_to_get_there.value: Fly KBV or bus/ferry; gateway to Railay, Ao Nang, and the Andaman islands.

local_transport.value: Songthaews, longtail boats (Railay is boat-access only), Grab limited, rented scooters.

value.name: Railay Beach

value.name: Tiger Cave Temple (Wat Tham Sua)

value.name: Ao Nang
value.why: Main beach town + boat hub.

value.name: Four Islands / Hong Islands tours
value.why: Classic longtail island-hopping.

value.name: Phi Phi Islands (from Krabi)
value.why: Same iconic islands; closer than Phuket for some tours.

value.name: Emerald Pool & Hot Springs
value.why: Natural warm pools in the jungle; great for a swim.

value.who_for: Main tourist hub, beachfront, dining, and nightlife; range of hotels.
Secluded, climbing, luxury resorts; only accessible by boat.

value.why: Seafood, grilled meats, and Thai snacks.
Spicier regional style.
Panoramic views and Thai fusion; book ahead.
Beachfront Thai food with a relaxed vibe.
Classic day trip from Krabi; speedboat 1–1.5 hrs.
Snorkeling and kayaking among limestone islands; 45 min boat.
Chill island with beaches and national park; 2 hrs by ferry+minivan.

how_to_get_there.value: Fly USM (Bangkok Airways hub; pricier) or fly to Surat Thani + ferry, or bus+ferry. GULF coast — different season from Andaman.

local_transport.value: Songthaews, taxis (negotiate), rented scooters; ring road circles the island.

value.name: Big Buddha (Wat Phra Yai)

value.name: Fisherman's Village (Bophut)

Famous for Full Moon Party but also has quiet beaches; 30 min ferry.

local_transport.value: Bicycle, tuk-tuk hire by the hour, Grab.

_open_questions._note: Genuine uncertainties / volatile / needs-official-source. Honesty over confidence.
value: VISA DAY-COUNT (priority): US visa-exemption stay is mid-change — 60 days now, Cabinet-approved 30 days not yet in force (Royal Gazette pending, late June 2026). Served live in prose / verify on travel date; never frozen. Official: travel.state.gov + thaievisa.go.th.
PROOF OF FUNDS: resolved to 20,000 THB/person, 40,000 THB/family for visa-exempt/tourist (NOT the 10k/20k VoA tier). Sporadically enforced + change-prone — verify before stating in prose.
CURRENCY DECLARATION LIMIT: sources disagreed (USD 15k vs 20k vs 450k THB). Left null/verify_recommended — confirm official threshold before any article states a number.
ADVISORY LEVEL: sources agree Level 1 + deep-south exception, but YMYL — confirm current US State Dept level + date when writing health-and-safety articles.
Change-prone fees to verify (not freeze): visa extension ~1,900 THB; ATM foreign-card fee ~220 THB; any 'tourist fee' proposals.
